Hi, some slow-moving packages stil require numpy < 2, that's why we keep it in openSUSE. We currently switched Python 3.13 build on and `core/tests/test_nditer.py::test_iter_refcount`, `lib/tests/test_utils.py::test_deprecate_help_indentation` and `lib/tests/test_utils.py::test_deprecate_preserve_whitespace` fail.

For `core/tests/test_nditer.py::test_iter_refcount` it was already noted in https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/26207. 

The failing tests in `lib/tests/test_utils.py` fail on the absence of leading whitespaces. From the What's new in Python 3.13:
```
The compiler now strips common leading whitespace from every line in a docstring.
```

Otherwise numpy 1.26.4 builds fine on Python3.13. I hope no other code changes than skipping those tests are needed. Could you please consider skipping the tests if it makes sense for you?

```
